English.question: Select the sentence with the strongest formal tone.
choices: Kids these days are glued to phones.
The prevalence of prolonged screen exposure warrants targeted digital well-being interventions.
Phones are kind of bad, I guess.

English.question: State one counter-argument to this claim: 'All school assessments should be open-book'.
Evaluate whether AI grammar tools should be allowed in graded essays. Give one support and one safeguard.
Identify the best topic sentence for a paragraph on public library relevance.
choices: Libraries still matter in modern cities because they provide equitable access to verified information, quiet study spaces, and community programmes.

English.question: Choose the most credible rebuttal sentence for an essay claiming 'homework should be abolished'.
choices: Homework is perfect and has no downsides.
Homework should stay because tradition matters.
A moderated homework load can reinforce retrieval practice when tasks are purposeful and feedback is timely.
Students dislike homework so it should continue.

English.question: State one hidden assumption in this claim: 'Co-curricular activities should be graded'.

Which introduction best frames a balanced argumentative essay on later school start times?
choices: School should start late because students are lazy.
Delaying school start times can improve adolescent learning outcomes, but implementation should consider transport and family constraints.
Late starts are always better in every country.

English.question: Evaluate a policy to cap homework time per subject. Give one likely benefit and one implementation problem.

Which thesis is most specific and arguable for an essay on AI in education?
choices: Schools should ban AI tools immediately.
AI-assisted learning should be permitted for drafting and revision, provided assessment safeguards preserve independent thinking.
